skill.name: create-pr
skill.description: Create GitHub pull requests that follow Sentry engineering conventions, including branch checks, PR descriptions, titles, issue references, and reviewer selection.
skill.summary: Prepare and create Sentry-style GitHub pull requests with clear descriptions and review context.
skill.author: getsentry

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and network patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirms the external commands are expected git and GitHub CLI workflow instructions, while the weak cryptography findings are false positives with no cryptographic code in the cited lines. The skill can create or edit remote pull requests, so it carries medium operational risk and should be used with authenticated GitHub accounts only.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated GitHub CLI Commands Can Change Remote State
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users or agents to run git and gh commands, including creating a pull request and adding reviewers. This is expected for the skill purpose, but mistakes can publish incomplete changes or notify reviewers from an authenticated account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly require authenticated gh access and show commands that create and modify pull requests. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it has clear external side effects.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution in PR Body Example
medium_findings.description: The PR creation example uses shell command substitution with a quoted here document to pass the body to gh. The quoted delimiter limits shell expansion inside the body, but agents should still avoid inserting unreviewed user text into shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is visible and semantically relevant. The here document is quoted, so the immediate injection risk is reduced, but command construction still deserves review.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged two hardcoded URLs. They point to Sentry engineering documentation and do not show data exfiltration, credential transmission, or unauthorized network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly documented reference links. No code path sends local data to those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines contain metadata, prose, PR examples, command text, or issue references. No evidence found for cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage. The alerts appear to match ordinary markdown text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains multiple fenced command examples for git and gh. These commands are expected, but users should confirm branch state and PR content before allowing an agent to run them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in the markdown and align with the skill's purpose. They are not malicious, but they can read repository state or perform authenticated GitHub actions.

content.value_statement: Teams need pull requests that explain intent, scope, and reviewer expectations clearly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Sentry-style PR creation.
actual_capabilities: Checks branch status and commits before PR creation.
Reviews commits and diffs against the main branch.
Drafts PR descriptions with motivation and reviewer context.
Creates GitHub pull requests through the gh CLI.
Adds individual or team reviewers when requested.
Uses Sentry issue reference conventions in PR bodies.
limitations: Requires an authenticated GitHub CLI installation.
Assumes the repository uses Sentry engineering conventions.
Does not validate CI results after PR creation.
Does not choose reviewers unless names or teams are known.

use_cases.title: Prepare a focused feature PR
use_cases.description: Summarize a completed feature branch and create a clear PR for review.

use_cases.title: Create a bug fix PR
use_cases.description: Explain the defect, the fix, and the issue reference before opening the PR.

use_cases.title: Standardize team review requests
use_cases.description: Apply consistent titles, descriptions, and reviewer assignments across a repository.

prompt_templates.title: Open a basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a ready branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pull request for my current branch using Sentry conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Draft before creating
prompt_templates.scenario: Review the proposed description before running gh.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my branch against main and draft the PR title and body before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Include issue context
prompt_templates.scenario: Connect a PR to a tracker issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PR for this branch and reference SENTRY-1234 in the body.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced reviewer workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Create the PR and request specific reviewers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare the PR, explain the riskiest review areas, then add reviewers alice and @getsentry/platform.

output_examples.input: Create a PR for a branch that adds Slack thread replies.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks branch status, reviews the diff, writes a concise description, and opens a GitHub PR with a Sentry-style title.

output_examples.input: Draft a PR body for a null-response bug fix.
output_examples.output: The assistant produces a short explanation of the bug, the change, and the linked issue without adding a test plan section.

output_examples.input: Add reviewers after creating the PR.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates the PR and requests the named people or team through GitHub CLI.

best_practices: Confirm all intended changes are committed before creating the PR.
Review the generated title and body before running gh commands.
Keep each PR focused on one feature, fix, or refactor.
anti_patterns: Do not bundle unrelated changes into one PR.
Do not create a PR from a dirty working tree.
Do not add large reviewer lists without clear ownership.

faq.question: Does this skill require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects gh to be installed and authenticated before PR creation.

faq.question: Can it create the PR automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes gh commands for creating a PR after reviewing branch state and changes.

faq.question: Does it write test plans?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly avoids test plan sections in PR descriptions.

faq.question: Can it add reviewers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add named users or teams when those reviewers are known.

faq.question: Is it only for Sentry repositories?
faq.answer: It is designed for Sentry conventions, but the workflow can help similar GitHub projects.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should review the branch diff, PR title, PR body, and reviewer list before running gh.
